metadata.title: Profile Web Research Evidence
metadata.purpose: Source-backed company, affiliation, ownership, contact, and trademark research captured in resumable batches.
metadata.reviewPolicy: Use official company, government, university, patent, PBR, or reputable transaction sources. Do not infer legal ownership from breeder employment alone.

profiles.ownershipType: strategic group subsidiary / affiliated breeding program
profiles.ownershipSummary: Founded by Arsene and Laurence Maillard and connected to Frutaria Innovation and Grupo SAMCA since 2018.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep separate from AC Fruit. The two French breeding programs were previously combined incorrectly in the alias configuration.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Created to continue Rene Monteux-Caillet's program; Star Fruits is identified as the exclusive commercialization organization.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve the registered holder shown on each older right; program lineage does not itself prove a later assignment.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Founders of Agro Selections Fruits; the operating program is now connected to Frutaria Innovation and Grupo SAMCA.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official company history identifies Arsene and Laurence Maillard as founders of the breeding program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep the names as breeder attribution, but do not treat them as a separate current acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Co-founder of Agro Selections Fruits and reported as continuing in management after the Frutaria investment.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Company and transaction history connect Laurence Maillard to Agro Selections Fruits.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Use as a named breeder within the ASF profile, not as a duplicate target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: AC Fruit states that it was formed to continue his breeding program; Star Fruits commercializes the varieties.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: AC Fruit's official history states that it took over and continued the Rene Monteux-Caillet program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Program lineage is verified, but old rights must retain the registered holder unless an assignment is documented.

profiles.ownershipSummary: B Q Genetics' history says Anderson sold his breeding business to Norman and Glen Bradford in 1981.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official B Q Genetics history identifies the Bradford program as successor to Anderson's breeding business.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as program lineage, not proof that every historical right was assigned. Not a current individual succession target.

profiles.ownershipType: deceased individual assignee / estate verification required
profiles.ownershipSummary: Lowell Glen Bradford died in 2022. B Q Genetics continues through Jon Quisenberry, while some active patents still name Bradford individually as assignee.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Operating-program continuity is verified, but active individually titled rights require assignment or estate evidence before consolidation.

webResearchSources.label: B Q Genetics history and contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority succession lead. Do not automatically move individually titled rights into B Q Genetics without assignment or estate evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: private family breeding program / succession special situation
profiles.ownershipSummary: The Bradford family acquired Fred Anderson's breeding business in 1981. Lowell Glen Bradford died in 2022 and Jon Quisenberry continues the program, while patents, estate interests, trademarks, and international licenses may sit in different legal perimeters.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Jon Quisenberry is identified as the continuing operator/breeder.
profiles.trademarkOwner: Lowell Glen Bradford for PLUMTASTIC; some international commercialization marks are held with or by partners
profiles.webResearchNotes: Highest-priority chain-of-title audit. Confirm BQ Genetics LP partners, Glen's estate or trust, patent-by-patent assignments, Jon's personally held rights, jurisdictional licenses, and authority to transfer PLUMTASTIC.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Fruit raspberry rights are associated with Allberry B.V./Advanced Berry Breeding, while ornamental hydrangea rights have different assignees.
profiles.candidateParent: Advanced Berry Breeding / Allberry B.V. for fruit records only
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Patent assignments differ by crop and must be rolled up at the record level.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not apply a global company rollup to all Niels Arts records.

profiles.ownershipSummary: University of Udine fruit-crop genetics professor associated with the Institute of Applied Genomics; relevant rights identify institutional holders.
profiles.parentCompany: University of Udine / Institute of Applied Genomics
profiles.webResearchNotes: Retain for licensing and relationship context, not as an individual acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official materials identify Cerasina GmbH as applicant and Peter Stoppel as breeder.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Cerasina's official contact and rights materials connect Peter Stoppel to the company.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll into Cerasina for sourcing while preserving breeder attribution.

profiles.ownershipSummary: German GmbH represented by Peter Stoppel. The company holds Prim cherry patents, while public trademark records identify Peter personally as owner of CERASINA and WEIGI.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Peter Stoppel is identified as the company breeder and contact.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Founder-dependent target. Confirm GmbH shareholders, licenses from Peter to the company, rootstock genetics, royalty ownership, and whether a transaction would include the CERASINA and WEIGI marks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Flevo Berry identifies Marcel Suiker as Owner / Breeder; official variety records identify Flevo Berry as applicant and holder.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official company and PBR sources connect Marcel Suiker to Flevo Berry.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll into Flevo Berry while retaining Marcel Suiker as the named owner/breeder contact.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official site identifies Flevo as an independent strawberry breeder and Marcel Suiker as Owner / Breeder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Marcel Suiker - Owner / Breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strong acquisition-screen candidate, subject to ownership, scale, and financial diligence.

profiles.ownershipType: public university breeder with private collaborations
profiles.ownershipSummary: Michigan State University breeder. Later Berry Blue collaborations are held by BB IP Repository LLC; records must be separated by assignee.
profiles.parentCompany: Michigan State University for university records; BB IP Repository LLC for specified collaborations
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not use one global company rollup. The person is not an individual acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Foundational IFG breeder. IFG rights now sit within Bloom Fresh, while earlier Sun World varieties must remain with Sun World when that is the recorded owner.
profiles.parentCompany: Bloom Fresh International for IFG program records
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not treat David Cain as a standalone target; split legacy Sun World and IFG/Bloom Fresh records by legal owner.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Sheehan Genetics became wholly owned by SNFL after Timothy Sheehan's death; the program is now within Bloom Fresh.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: SNFL history and later government transfer records connect the Sheegene program to Bloom Fresh.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Historical program lineage, not a current standalone acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Associated with the ARRA grape program now held and commercialized through Grapa Global.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Current official rights identify Grapa Global as holder and Sal Giumarra as a breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll into Grapa Global while preserving breeder attribution.

profiles.ownershipType: private family-connected breeding and licensing company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Holder and commercial platform for the ARRA grape program, connected to the Giumarra and Karniel families.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely above the target size range, but ownership and succession structure remain relevant for relationship mapping.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Varieties were developed at HortResearch and assigned to that institute; the successor organization is within New Zealand's government-owned Bioeconomy Science Institute.
profiles.parentCompany: Bioeconomy Science Institute / Plant & Food Research
profiles.webResearchNotes: Licensing/relationship signal only, not an acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Normalize to Takashi Haji. Official NARO materials identify him in Japan's public fruit-breeding program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Government program record; do not rank as an individual acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Focused Dutch F1 hybrid strawberry seed breeder acquired by Planasa.
profiles.trademarkOwner: ABZ Aardbeien uit Zaad Holding BV
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strong focused IP and brand evidence, but already acquired by Planasa and therefore not a standalone target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Integrated cultivar development, nursery, fruit production, marketing, and IP-rights manager reporting more than 70 PBR varieties and 130 provisional varieties.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely benchmark or strategic partner rather than a clean small-company acquisition target. No live USPTO mark tied to Stargrow was verified in this pass.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Apple and pear commercialization company with institutional breeding relationships; some brands have different or co-owners.
profiles.trademarkOwner: Prevar Limited for multiple marks; exceptions and co-owners exist
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not assume every consumer brand is owned solely by Prevar; confirm each mark and institutional agreement during diligence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: UK soft-fruit breeder and propagator with major retailer supply relationships.
profiles.webResearchNotes: No live USPTO marks for the named berry brands were verified; run a UKIPO search before treating them as registered assets.

profiles.ownershipType: private breeding company with institutional co-ownership links
profiles.ownershipSummary: Fruit breeding and licensing company with Wageningen/WUR relationships; some marks or rights are co-owned.
profiles.trademarkOwner: Fresh Forward Holding B.V.; some records include Wageningen Plant Research
profiles.webResearchNotes: Trademark evidence is real, but institutional co-ownership and control must be mapped mark by mark.

profiles.ownershipSummary: VIF manages Proseplan's breeding, varieties, and brands with support from founding nurseries Augusta Viveros and Viveros Veron; retain VIF and Proseplan as legal-name aliases.
profiles.webResearchNotes: One operating profile, but preserve V.I.F. S.A.R.L. and Viveros Proseplan S.L. on the underlying legal records.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Spanish strawberry breeder formed in 2018. Public corporate and industry sources identify Luis Maria Aliseda Martin as the company administrator and owner/technical director, but do not establish the current cap table.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Luis Maria Aliseda Martin - public administrator and owner/technical director; Tom Rogers is also a public commercial contact.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority candidate. Confirm current shareholder percentages, the reason for surrendered Spanish PVRs, the survival of private licenses, non-Spanish rights, and direct EUIPO trademark status.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Active French SARL with a small team. Marie-Laure Eteve and Pierre Riou are current co-managers; public filing history shows family/company-security transfers but no verified outside acquisition.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Marie-Laure Eteve and Pierre Riou - co-managers.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority candidate. Confirm the current cap table and title-by-title separation between COT-owned genetics and varieties that COT edits, licenses, or represents for third parties.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Large cooperative with roughly 200 member-producers, extensive clone/genetics operations, and a broad commercial variety catalog.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Too large and structurally difficult for the current acquisition target; retain for partnership and licensing context.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Nelson-family-led company with Simplot investment; its 2024 consolidation brought affiliated breeding programs including Berry Genetics together.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Above the current target range. The company reports more than 50 patents/PBR certificates; dashboard records are jurisdictional rights, not unique cultivar counts.

profiles.ownershipSummary: MBG identifies Berry Blue as its proprietary genetics program and operates within Naturipe; BB IP Repository is a related rights-holding name.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not a standalone breeder acquisition target; retain for licensing, partnership, and rights-holder mapping.

profiles.ownershipType: private breeding platform with institutional ownership history
profiles.ownershipSummary: Allberry B.V. served as the raspberry IP/licensing entity for Advanced Berry Breeding; acquired through the Planasa shareholders' investment platform in 2021.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Current ultimate ownership after the EW/Planasa transaction needs corporate-document confirmation.

profiles.ownershipType: jointly controlled private variety licensing vehicle
profiles.ownershipSummary: A Dutch court decision states that Mang B.V. and Onubafruit S.C.A. each own 50% of FV. It also describes a transfer lock-up through 2033 and an Onubafruit consent requirement.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Public company contacts include Corneel de Kort, Borja Sanchez, and Marieke van Uffelen.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not a straightforward founder-owned target. Confirm Dutch registry details, shareholder agreement restrictions, change-of-control rights, and the separation of Agriom-owned versus FV-owned germplasm and know-how.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Large international breeding and nursery group owned by EW Group; Darbonne is an acquired historical breeding/holder name.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Benchmark only for current acquisition size. Preserve Darbonne as the legal name on historical records.

profiles.ownershipType: private family holding company / special situation
profiles.ownershipSummary: California holding company formed in October 2024 with Brad and Casey Jones as managers. Patent records show a two-step transfer from Prima Wawona's liquidating trust to Sun Valley Packing and then to Threefold.
profiles.parentCompany: Jones family / Sun Valley Packing relationship
profiles.leadershipSummary: Brad Jones and Casey Jones - public managers.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority assignment audit. Obtain LLC member information, bankruptcy asset-purchase schedules, germplasm and license schedules, and the instrument supporting an August 2024 effective date that predates Threefold's October 2024 formation.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Plant & Food Research and predecessor HortResearch are now part of New Zealand's Bioeconomy Science Institute.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll up HortResearch and Horticulture and Food Research Institute names. Relationship/licensing context only.

profiles.canonicalName: The James Hutton Institute Scientific Services
profiles.ownershipSummary: Commercial scientific-services arm of The James Hutton Institute.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not a conventional acquisition target; retain for soft-fruit licensing relationships.

profiles.ownershipSummary: NIAB incorporates the historical East Malling fruit-breeding program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll up East Malling Research and NIAB EMR aliases; relationship/licensing context only.

profiles.ownershipType: private berry breeding company / nominee-holder relationship
profiles.ownershipSummary: Medium-confidence operating rollup connecting IQ Berries, Peter Rolfe, and Rolfe Nominees Pty Ltd.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Confirm the legal relationship between the operating brand and Rolfe Nominees before outreach.

profiles.ownershipType: private fruit operating company / legacy brand relationship
profiles.ownershipSummary: PRIMOR FRUIT domains redirect to S.A.T. Sintesis, which presents the current Primor fruit program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat PRIMOR as a legacy or related entity; confirm the exact legal ownership chain before consolidation of rights.

profiles.ownershipSummary: International blueberry genetics and commercialization platform associated with Next Progeny, OZblu, and United Exports.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely above the target range; retain Next Progeny as a legal-name alias.

profiles.ownershipSummary: California strawberry nursery with public variety and company-history pages.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential nursery/breeding acquisition screen; ownership and financial scale remain to be verified.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Washington nursery offering apple, cherry, pear, peach, and related tree-fruit cultivars.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential nursery acquisition screen; verify whether it owns meaningful breeding IP versus distributing third-party cultivars.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Grower-led apple improvement association founded by Mitch Lynd and Ed Fackler; current contact materials identify Bill Dodd as president.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bill Dodd - President; Mitch Lynd and Ed Fackler - founders.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely a relationship/licensing opportunity rather than a conventional acquisition.

profiles.ownershipSummary: No verified operating website or LinkedIn page; similarly named domains were rejected as parked.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Possible SNFL relationship needs holder-level evidence. Keep website blank.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Associated with Steve/S.M. Southwick records; the apparent company domain is parked or inactive.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank; verify the current legal entity and operator through assignment or registry evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Focused apricot rights holder; the apparent official domain is under construction.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank in the profile until a useful operating site or registry-backed contact is available.

profiles.ownershipSummary: No verified official operating website; similarly named domains belonged to unrelated businesses.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank. Use CPVO evidence until a correct official source is found.

profiles.ownershipSummary: No verified operating website or LinkedIn page; the apparent domain did not resolve.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank and rely on patent/PBR evidence pending legal-entity research.

profiles.ownershipType: large private multigenerational grower / breeding platform
profiles.ownershipSummary: Zaninovich-family company with extensive proprietary grape breeding and more than 1,000 employees.
profiles.trademarkOwner: Sunview Marketing International; older SUNVIEW records also identify Marko Zaninovich
profiles.webResearchNotes: Too large for the current acquisition target. Audit current walnut-attributed records separately for possible misattribution.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Spanish company focused on strawberry and raspberry breeding; no verified parent acquisition was found in the current pass.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential standalone target. Confirm current shareholders, financial scale, and title ownership before outreach.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Henri and Arnaud Delbard formed the current company in 2012 to restart the historical Delbard varietal-research business.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Henri Delbard and Arnaud Delbard - family leadership identified in official history.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Aggregate historical PRGD records for sourcing context while preserving the legal assignee on each right. Keep the breeding nursery separate from Delbard-branded retail garden centers.

profiles.ownershipSummary: New Zealand company formerly named Brookfield N.Z. Limited and identified as breeder and licensor of the Baigent apple program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: No verified standalone operating website was found. Use the official register as the primary entity source.

profiles.ownershipType: private grower, shipper, breeder, and rights holder
profiles.ownershipSummary: California strawberry company appearing as applicant, holder, and assignee on protected varieties.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Correct source parsing that renders the name as Inc. Sweet Darling Sales. Keep Larse Farms separate unless a record establishes the legal relationship.

profiles.ownershipType: active private orchard and nursery group
profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical Domaine de Castang SA and current Domaine de Castang SAS can be grouped when address and corporate history align; SCICA Castang is a separate marketing cooperative.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep legal entities separate at rights-holder level, with an operating-group view for sourcing.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Autonomous public agricultural research entity of the Generalitat Valenciana with its own legal personality.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge with Spain's INIA. Records naming both should retain separate co-holders.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Agricultural research institution of the Principality of Asturias with plant genetic-resource programs in apples, beans, hazelnuts, blueberries, and grapes.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Institutional and non-acquisition profile.

profiles.ownershipType: strategic seed company within an agricultural cooperative
profiles.ownershipSummary: International seed company owned by Limagrain, a French agricultural cooperative.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep HM.CLAUSE and Vilmorin-Mikado as separate operating profiles even when an ultimate-parent view aggregates them under Limagrain.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Armstrong Nurseries is a historical patent assignee. The operating successor is employee-owned Armstrong Garden Centers, with Armstrong Growers as a current growing division.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not represent old Armstrong Nurseries patents as current Armstrong Garden Centers assets without assignment and expiration checks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Russian public breeding institute associated with Evgenij Nikolaevich Sedov and numerous apple and pear varieties.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll up Sedov-authored VNIISPK records for institutional context while preserving the named breeder on each record.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Fruit-genetics researcher associated with the University of Udine and the Istituto di Genomica Applicata; rights should be rolled up only where the applicant or assignee identifies the institution.
profiles.parentCompany: University of Udine / Istituto di Genomica Applicata
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not infer legal ownership from employment. Apply the institutional rollup record by record.

profiles.ownershipSummary: USDA breeder whose plant patent records identify the United States, represented by the Secretary of Agriculture, as assignee.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Institutional and non-acquisition profile.

profiles.ownershipType: breeder with mixed public and private assignees
profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical records identify the Regents of the University of California, while later records identify California Berry Cultivars LLC.
profiles.candidateParent: University of California or California Berry Cultivars LLC, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Applicant and assignee differ across the portfolio and must be evaluated record by record.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not globally roll Douglas Shaw into the University of California. Split records between UC and California Berry Cultivars based on applicant or assignee.

profiles.ownershipSummary: University of Arkansas fruit breeder who led the public program from 1997 through 2023.
profiles.parentCompany: University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture
profiles.webResearchNotes: Institutional and non-acquisition profile.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Initiated the University of Arkansas fruit-breeding program in 1964.
profiles.parentCompany: University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture
profiles.webResearchNotes: Institutional and non-acquisition profile.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The name can refer to multiple French nonprofit conservation organizations and cannot be safely resolved without an address, association identifier, or filing date.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge from name alone. Both likely are nonprofit conservation organizations rather than acquisition targets, but the exact holder remains quarantined.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Cornell Research Foundation was Cornell's IP-management subsidiary. Cornell University Foundation is a separate philanthropic donor-advised fund, so this spelling must not be silently merged.
profiles.candidateParent: Cornell University or Cornell University Foundation, source record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The exact source label appears misspelled and could point to two legally distinct entities.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Quarantine until the original record, address, and legal entity are confirmed.

profiles.ownershipType: private family agricultural and breeding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Family company founded in 1977. Its official site describes fully proprietary strawberry varieties, while source records use both S.A. and S.L. legal forms.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strong private-family sourcing signal. Confirm the current legal entity, shareholder structure, portfolio title, and scale.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Marionnet Label is the R&D division of Groupe Agri Finest Company and continues the historical Marionnet/CRIV berry breeding program.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely above the small-target range. Preserve Marionnet, Marionnet Label, and CRIV legal names on underlying records while presenting the current group context.

profiles.ownershipType: private family-run grower and breeding group
profiles.ownershipSummary: John Davies founded the strawberry business in the early 1990s. The current public operating entity is S&A Group Holdings Ltd., with a documented cultivar R&D program.
profiles.leadershipSummary: John Davies - founder identified in official history.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Real private breeder and grower, but likely larger than the current target range. Confirm group ownership, EBITDA scale, and separability of breeding assets.

profiles.ownershipType: private breeder and direct-to-consumer plant company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Swiss company represented by Markus Kobelt with verified plant-patent and operating-site evidence.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Markus Kobelt - company representative and breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Verified breeder profile. Likely broader and larger than the current target, but retain for ownership and portfolio benchmarking.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Focused apple clone and variety company founded by South Tyrolean nurseries and associated with the Gala Schniga portfolio.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Consortium structure may make this more suitable for partnership or licensing than a control acquisition.

profiles.ownershipType: small private founder-led breeding and IP company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Bill and Erica Lynch created Fashion Foods to manage plant-breeding programs, intellectual property, and brands. The public site describes eight apple varieties.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bill Lynch and Erica Lynch - founders.
profiles.websiteCultivarCountBasis: Official site states eight apple varieties
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority acquisition-fit candidate: small, private, breeder-led, contactable, and explicitly focused on IP and brands. Confirm shareholder ownership, revenue, license obligations, and the Roxy commercialization structure.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Spanish cooperative with strawberry and blueberry application evidence; no working official contact page was found in this pass.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Cooperative structure lowers acquisition fit. Retain for licensing and source relationships while verifying a public contact path.

profiles.ownershipSummary: No official operating site was verified; obvious domains did not resolve or connect.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank. Use patent and regulator evidence until the corporate entity and current operator are verified.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Potential Australian apple-IP company; no reliable official operating site was verified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential small-company lead. Keep website blank pending Australian registry and current-operator verification.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Possible Washington apple-IP company; the apparent company domain was parked and rejected.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank and verify legal entity, current operator, and assignment status before treating as actionable.

profiles.ownershipSummary: No official public site was verified. The obvious German Proplant domain belongs to an unrelated agriculture software company.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Low priority pending active-right and registry checks. Keep the website blank.

profiles.ownershipType: private South African citrus rights holder unresolved
profiles.ownershipSummary: Citrus IP signal associated with Ruby Valencia and Ruby SL; no official current operating site was verified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep website blank pending South African registry or authoritative industry evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Legacy entity derived from the former Plant Breeding Institute, Cambridge, which was divided and sold to Unilever in 1987.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as a legacy/dormant rights-holder context, not a current acquisition target. The obvious domain is parked and must not be shown as a company website.

profiles.ownershipType: private rights manager with controlling parent
profiles.ownershipSummary: Berryplants Holdings Limited obtained 75% or greater control in 2023. Meiosis manages and protects more than 800 soft-fruit varieties and selections, which are not necessarily owned assets.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Philip Armstrong and Sandra Starling - current Companies House directors.
profiles.websiteCultivarCountBasis: Managed varieties and selections, not owned rights
profiles.webResearchNotes: Rights-management capability target rather than an independent breeder acquisition. Separate owned, represented, and managed rights before valuing the portfolio.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Terrena subsidiary formed through the merger of Pepinieres du Valois and Davodeau Ligonniere; IFO is its fruit research arm.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Emmanuel de Lapparent and Jean-Rene Gourdon are identified in the 2026 leadership transition.
profiles.websiteCultivarCountBasis: IFO displayed varieties; owned-versus-managed rights unresolved
profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve IFO, Valois, and Davodeau Ligonniere on legal records. Benchmark or partner only for the current target range.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Active Australian private company and originating owner of M7 citrus rights. US title was assigned to Chislett Developments while other rights remain under Chislett Investments.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge Chislett Investments and Chislett Developments. Confirm directors, shareholders, territorial title, licenses, and M7 trademark ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Family-run Manitoba nursery associated with the Ronald family and more than forty introductions, including apple and pear cultivars.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Breeding personnel include Rick Durand, Philip Ronald, and Michel Touchette.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Credible family and succession profile, but verify fruit-only revenue, current ownership, directly owned rights, and Northern Garden trademark status.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Founded in 2003 and described as wholly owned by Graham Spencer. Handles PVR applications, licensing, and royalty collection rather than producing plants.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Graham Spencer - founder and owner.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential capability acquisition, but the disclosed portfolio is overwhelmingly ornamental. Confirm fruit share, royalty base, client concentration, contract assignability, and PFE B.V. ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: private single-asset rights vehicle with litigation risk
profiles.ownershipSummary: PVR and patent owner of PLBAR B1/Barnsby/Early Cripps Pink and party to ongoing CPVO litigation involving underlying Cripps rights.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential single-asset target with elevated authorization and litigation risk. Pink Lady trademark ownership is not established as a Teak asset.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Owned by more than twenty French nurserymen and active as exclusive worldwide publisher of certain INRAE fruit varieties; Quartier Neuf remains a separate co-breeder and rights contributor.
profiles.leadershipSummary: David Ray is identified as website publication officer.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely partnership rather than control acquisition. Confirm member cap table, INRAE change-of-control provisions, and title by variety.

profiles.ownershipType: large private family orchard and exporter
profiles.ownershipSummary: Hoddy-family orchard associated with Heartland Group; combined operations report more than NZD 50 million of sales.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Richard, Susan, Matthew, and Tristram Hoddy are associated with the active company.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Above target size. A historical PVR transferred to Plant & Food Research and should not be counted as current Vailima ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: private berry genetics company with strategic ownership ties
profiles.ownershipSummary: United Exports acquired Peter Rolfe's interests in Early Blue and OZ Peach; David Mazzardis retained an interest.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Confirm current cap table and rights held by Early Blue, Biza Trading, Prunus Persica, and OZ Varieties.

profiles.ownershipType: private tissue-culture and plant genetics company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Bulgarian business focused primarily on Paulownia and forestry genetics, with goji, berry, and fruit-rootstock development activity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Adjacent rather than fruit-focused. Exclude the unrelated Malaysian Bio Tree Biotechnology LinkedIn profile.

profiles.ownershipType: private founder-led cherry originator and brand owner
profiles.ownershipSummary: Family-owned Wenatchee cherry originator that owns the Tip Top cultivar and Skylar Rae trademark, with global commercialization arrangements involving Snyder LLC and C&O Nursery.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Troy Toftness - public founder/operator lead.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority target. Confirm LLC membership, royalty economics, territory performance, license assignability, trademark jurisdictions, and partner consent rights.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical Alexandra, New Zealand breeding program of John McLaren; Nevis Fruit later became rightsholder for Nevson/Sonya.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge automatically into Nevis. A current unrelated New Zealand company uses the same name. Resolve the historical entity and title chain.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Active New Zealand company with public directors John McLaren and Pierre Riou and reported shareholder participation from the McLaren family and French partners.
profiles.leadershipSummary: John McLaren and Pierre Riou - directors.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority succession candidate. Confirm direct contact, full active portfolio, trademark status, licenses, revenue, and succession plans.

profiles.canonicalName: Green and Red Apple Pty Ltd.
profiles.ownershipSummary: Australian PBR holder for RDS; US patent title was assigned to Fruit Varieties International, which acts as agent in some territories.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not merge into FVI. Resolve entity ownership, worldwide title, FVI agreements, Joya trademark ownership, and royalty economics.

profiles.canonicalName: LFP Lizenzen fuer Fruechte und Pflanzen GmbH
profiles.ownershipSummary: Standalone licensing company with a documented relationship to Hilmar Graeb's sweet-cherry breeding program; no shareholder relationship to Graeb is established.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potentially attractive small licensor. Confirm shareholders, employee count, financials, and title to each KIR selection.

profiles.ownershipType: private founder-succession cherry licensing special situation
profiles.ownershipSummary: PFV (Contracts) LLC appears as owner in USDA records and IVU as representative; current commercialization operates through the Meda Cherry portfolio after founder Marvin Nies's death.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority special situation. Confirm LLC members, economics, founder succession, and patent-by-patent boundaries between PFV, PFV Contracts, and IVU.

profiles.ownershipType: private family nursery and varietal-development group
profiles.ownershipSummary: Mercier Freres, Pepinieres Mercier, and Mercier Novatech operate within the same French family group, with Novatech focused on varietal development.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely above target size and primarily viticulture. Confirm family cap table, consolidated financials, and the legal split of rights between Mercier Freres and Novatech.

profiles.ownershipSummary: PRUMAPY owns 99.65% of Star Fruits Diffusion and serves as the holding company for the group's founding nursery families.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Renaud Pierson - managing director; Melanie Bordet - head of varieties division.
profiles.websiteCultivarCountBasis: Approximate represented portfolio; not a count of owned rights
profiles.webResearchNotes: Established network likely above target size. Distinguish group-owned rights from represented varieties and third-party consumer marks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Boomkwekerijen Henri Fleuren is the operating nursery and Han Fleuren Holding appears as an IP-holding entity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Attractive family platform. Confirm holding-company chain, EBITDA, succession, and owned-versus-licensed variety inventory.

profiles.ownershipSummary: AGV Innovation & Varieties is an R&D and IP vehicle managed within Fruitist, formerly Agrovision.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Billion-dollar-scale strategic company. Retain AGV as a legal IP vehicle but exclude from the acquisition target universe.

profiles.ownershipSummary: UK filings show Emla Ltd controlling the operating company, with the James family controlling the upper holding structure.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Credible family and succession candidate, but production scale may exceed the target range. Confirm EBITDA and owned-versus-licensed catalog varieties.

profiles.ownershipType: small private focused variety-creation and IP company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Active French SAS with a reported 1-2 employee profile, led by Daniel Sauvaitre and Jean-Emmanuel Champeix; breeder Jean-Marie Lespinasse died in 2025.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Daniel Sauvaitre and Jean-Emmanuel Champeix - public leaders.
profiles.webResearchNotes: One of the strongest acquisition candidates: small scale, focused IP, and succession dynamics. Confirm shareholders, contact details, full rights list, and exclusivity terms.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Approximately forty-person Australian family nursery with some owned PBR material and substantial genetics licensed from ANFIC and PlantNet.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Plausible family target, but separate owned PBR from third-party licenses and broad ornamental inventory. Attach Colin James only on records explicitly naming JFT.

profiles.ownershipSummary: EB-series records involve Prunus Persica, Rolfe Nominees, and later Biza Trading; NS-series records identify Next Progeny.
profiles.candidateParent: Prunus Persica, Biza Trading, or Next Progeny, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Assignees differ across the breeder's portfolio and must be mapped record by record.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not create a global Mazzardis company rollup. Corporate relationships and the full assignment chain remain unresolved.

profiles.ownershipType: private founder-led nursery, farm, and breeding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Focused blueberry nursery and breeding platform with no parent found; international licensing partners do not establish ownership.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bob Gabriel - founder and president; Adam Wagner - breeding manager; Jon Horton - corporate development.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strong acquisition profile. Confirm cap table, revenue and EBITDA, patent schedule, territorial licenses, and registered OBG trademarks.

profiles.ownershipType: small private family kiwifruit breeding company with litigation risk
profiles.ownershipSummary: Registry mirror reports Gary Baker as director and a family share structure. Litigation with Zespri concerns E2 development and distribution.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Gary Russell Baker - public director.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Succession-scale opportunity with major title, distinctness, and litigation risk. Recheck ownership against the primary Companies Office before reliance.

profiles.ownershipType: private nursery and agricultural operating group
profiles.ownershipSummary: Blueberry nursery, farm, agricultural-products, and winery operations publicly associated with Ken Patterson; licensed propagation must not be counted as owned IP.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Ken Patterson - publicly described owner; Jerod Gross - operating executive.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential operating target, but low confidence as an IP owner. Resolve legal entities, cap table, proprietary genetics, and the winery/nursery ownership structure.

profiles.ownershipType: small private apple breeder and IP vehicle
profiles.ownershipSummary: Howell TC2 patent records assignment to Howell Fruit Advantage LLC; TC2 and TC3 are commercialized as Lucy Rose and Lucy Glo through Proprietary Variety Management.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority small IP vehicle. Verify legal status, beneficial ownership, succession, current assignments, PVM economics, and official trademark ownership.

profiles.ownershipType: small private active cherry IP vehicle
profiles.ownershipSummary: US entity and nursery records point to Florida and California identities; Chilean material identifies Nipama as breeder and owner of ES 1952 with ANA Chile as representative.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Promising active cherry target. Resolve Florida-versus-California identity, managers and owners, ANA economics, filing coverage, and replacement trademark strategy.

profiles.ownershipType: small private family and trust-owned IP company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Registry mirror reports Robert Basil Mawley as director and a family/trust share structure. Waimea Variety Management handles non-New Zealand commercialization but is not the owner.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Robert Basil Mawley - public director.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority family IP holder. Contact is the commercialization manager, not ownership. Confirm current registry filings, royalty schedule, change-of-control terms, protection life, and APAL approvals.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Restricted share eligibility and grower control make corporate control unavailable; future breeding commercialization includes a 50/50 venture with Plant & Food Research.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Jason Te Brake - chief executive.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Exclude from acquisition universe. Retain for licensing, partnership, and competitive benchmarking.

profiles.ownershipSummary: South African operating business within public Dole plc. Preserve literal rights-holder names until each is matched to the local Pty or another Dole entity.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Hannes Nieuwoudt - managing director.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic-scale and controlled. Map each PVR to the exact Dole subsidiary before consolidating ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Spanish berry grower cooperative rather than a conventional equity-owned company.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Francisco Jose Gomez - president; Juan Francisco Banez - director general.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Control acquisition is unlikely. Verify registered owners of Cupla and consumer marks for licensing and distribution work.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Westfalia Fruit Estates is a specific subsidiary within Hans Merensky Holdings and should remain the legal-holder node where named.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Chris Bush - Westfalia group chief executive; Dr Khotso Mokhele - HMH chair.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Exclude from acquisition universe. Map PVR and trademark title to the correct subsidiary after reorganizations.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Government records show a 100% transfer of Ruby SL from Crocodile Valley Citrus Company to Crocodile Valley Farms on September 3, 2024; separate farm assets had moved in 2018.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential royalty shell. Confirm company ownership, assignment chain, license revenue, and why the PVR transfer followed the farm-asset sale by six years.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Industry association managed under the South African Subtropical Growers' Association umbrella, not an equity-owned breeding company.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Derek Donkin - chief executive.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not an acquisition target. Verify whether any specific record reflects ownership, research funding, applicant status, or representation.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Owned by the British Columbia Fruit Growers' Association and represents breeders and rights owners across apples, cherries, and berries.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Partnership target, not straightforward control acquisition. Separate varieties owned by SVC from those it represents under license.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical Caudle/Cameo rights vehicle reportedly inactive since 2015; the original US plant patent expired in 2013.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Legacy or orphan-rights investigation only. Confirm foreign PVR expiry, dissolution asset distribution, surviving marks, and any successor licensing entity.

profiles.ownershipSummary: CFIA records place Qualiagro at the same Casablanca address used by Les Domaines Agricoles, demonstrating operational affiliation but not proving legal parentage.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Shared official address and SweetCott commercialization context; cap table remains unverified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic-scale relationship and licensing watch. Confirm legal ownership, trademarks, and the exact Qualiagro/Les Domaines relationship.

profiles.ownershipSummary: KIWIKO publicly manages Yanoon genetics but is not a verified parent. Public breeding-station material describes a substantial seedling and comparison-variety program.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Zhongyan Wang is identified as breeder of Yanoon 7 in Japan's official record.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Licensing watch. Chinese ownership, team, trademarks, and economics remain opaque; KIWIKO contact is a manager path, not ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Public Aragon institution associated with the late almond breeder Rafael Socias i Company and protected almond varieties.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Institutional technology-transfer opportunity only, not an acquisition or personal succession target.

profiles.ownershipType: grower-owned cooperative with strategic partner ties
profiles.ownershipSummary: KG Growers evolved into Berry Gardens Growers; the cooperative sold its marketing company and packhouse to Driscoll's in 2023 after an earlier joint venture.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Patrick Bastow - managing and sales director; Robin Walker - chairman.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Industry relationship only, not control acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipType: unresolved or potentially removed legal entity
profiles.ownershipSummary: No verified operating business profile. A 2023 notice stated an intention to remove the company but does not prove completed removal.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress from actionable scoring until legal status, ownership, and the original avocado PVR records are obtained.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical owner of Powell Navel orange rights; the US patent expired in 2007 and UCR reports current proprietary association with Limoneira.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Archive as a legacy holder. Remove unrelated non-citrus attribution unless independently sourced.

profiles.ownershipType: private focused apple rights and trademark vehicle
profiles.ownershipSummary: South Australian company associated with a Royal Gala-derived apple and the live MIAPPLE trademark; no public ownership record was verified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Focused cultivar plus brand may be attractive. Confirm owners, licenses, royalties, and commercial volume.

profiles.ownershipType: legacy or potentially dissolved apple rights vehicle
profiles.ownershipSummary: Historical holder of CABp Fuji/Fuji Supreme; the US patent expired in 2025 and New Zealand notices indicated proposed removal.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Verify dissolution and surviving international rights before retaining as a succession lead.

profiles.ownershipType: private citrus royalty vehicle with manager relationship
profiles.ownershipSummary: Public records identify Comoset as holder of Leanri mandarin while Citrogold/BioGold manages commercialization; Citrogold now describes itself as part of Sun World.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential long-duration royalty vehicle. Confirm beneficial ownership, commercialization agreements, geographic exclusivity, and change-of-control provisions.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Seminis is a Bayer vegetable-seed business operating across more than twenty crops and 150 countries.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic competitor and licensing counterparty, not an acquisition target. Map legacy rights to current Bayer entities before consolidation.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Operating entity within publicly traded Constellation Brands; grapevine rights are relevant only for assignment and divestiture monitoring.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Nicholas Fink - chief executive from April 2026.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Monitor IP divestitures only; do not count abandoned LACAZ-related marks as live protection.

profiles.ownershipType: private multigenerational family orchard and rights holder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Nova Scotia orchard founded by Andy and Gail Parker with a visible transition toward daughter Janet Chappel; approximately one hundred orchard acres are reported.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Andy Parker and Gail Parker - founders; Janet Chappel - next-generation operating signal.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Highest-priority family target. Verify ownership, financial scale, commercialization, trademarks, and family objectives.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Austrian GmbH owned by AGRANA Fruit SAS; no specialist breeding program was independently confirmed.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Ralf Neumerkel and Markus Wagner - public directors.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Exclude from acquisition universe. Treat Manthra strawberry attribution as legacy title-holder evidence unless breeding activity is confirmed.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Tokyo Standard-listed company operating summer-strawberry breeding, nursery, and fruit marketing.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Iwao Takahashi - founder and chair; company president listed on the official profile.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential licensing partner, but public and outside the intended acquisition profile.

profiles.ownershipType: private founder-concentrated fruit-tree nursery and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Japanese fruit-tree nursery centered on representative director and breeder Shigetaka Sakurai.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Shigetaka Sakurai - representative director and breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority succession investigation. Confirm ownership, revenue, bench depth, trademarks, and international licensing.

profiles.ownershipType: acquired strategic berry genetics and propagation platform
profiles.ownershipSummary: Mexican berry genetics and propagation company acquired by Frutura in 2024 and now supporting Sun Belle's berry strategy.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Patricio Cortes - public chief executive.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Already acquired; transaction precedent and competitor only.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Swiss operating entity within public Fresh Del Monte, with active pineapple R&D and proprietary variety brands.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic competitor; monitor assignments and divestitures only.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The apparent Texas Premier Nursery website is unrelated. Relevant citrus rights instead trace to Craig Robert Pressler, C&B Pressler Pty Ltd as trustee, and 2PH Farms Ltd.
profiles.candidateParent: Pressler family citrus entities, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: CFIA and patent evidence identifies Australian Pressler entities rather than the Texas nursery.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress the unrelated Texas site. Rebuild record by record around the Pressler family entities and 2PH Farms.
